video:video.video:title: Bill Maher says 'Mark Twain' prize fits
video:video.video:description: As comedian Bill Maher picks up the Mark Twain Prize for American Humor, he explains why comparing him to the iconic Twain makes sense.

video:video.video:title: US ‘light artist’ opens his most daring skyspace yet
video:video.video:description: A monumental artwork based on Rome's Pantheon has been unveiled in Denmark. The dome by American “light artist” James Turrell is described as his most daring skyspace yet.

video:video.video:title: Janet Jackson, Teyana Taylor and Lizzo arrive at BETs
video:video.video:description: Janet Jackson, Teyana Taylor, Jamie Foxx, Common, Keke Palmer and Lizzo were among the celebrities in attendance at the BET ceremony. (June 29)

video:video.video:title: Inmates take control of a North Carolina jail after overpowering correctional staff
video:video.video:description: Inmates overpowered correctional staff and took over parts of a jail in eastern North Carolina early Monday, but the siege ended hours later when law enforcement officers entered the facility and seized control.

video:video.video:title: Alex Murdaugh's true crime saga continues as he's back in court for hearing on his murder retrial
video:video.video:description: South Carolina Judge Debra R. McCaslin has set an April 5 date to begin the retrial of disgraced lawyer Alex Murdaugh on two counts of murder in the shooting deaths of his wife and son.

video:video.video:title: Trump calls bill to address housing affordability ‘a yawn’ and says he doesn’t know if he’ll sign it
video:video.video:description: House Speaker Mike Johnson said over the weekend he would send the bill to the president on Monday anyway.

video:video.video:title: Stars leave Clive Davis' funeral
video:video.video:description: Stevie Wonder, Bruce Springsteen, Usher and Alicia Keys were amongst the celebrities attending Clive Davis' funeral. The music mogul died last week at age 94.

video:video.video:title: Jimmy Jam on who could play Prince in a biopic
video:video.video:description: Jimmy Jam weighs in on who could play Prince in a potential biopic.

video:video.video:title: Tri-state area expected to endure dangerous heat wave through end of holiday week
video:video.video:description: People were seen cooling off at Coney Island beach on Monday as officials have already placed New York under extreme heat watch.

video:video.video:title: Trump calls Supreme Court mail ballot ruling 'detrimental to honest elections'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump slammed the Supreme Court's mail ballot ruling Monday as "detrimental to honest elections" while praising a separate decision expanding his presidential authority to fire independent agency officials.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Warming climate, pollution and unplanned growth push Kashmir’s lakes toward disappearance
video:video.video:description: According to researchers, climate change-driven heat and unpredictable rainfall, combined with unplanned urban growth, have caused nearly half of the lakes in the mountainous, India-administered Jammu and Kashmir region to disappear or become highly polluted over the past six decades.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of people in northern Lebanon celebrate Brazil's victory over Japan
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people in northern Lebanon celebrated Brazil's 2-1 victory over Japan in the FIFA World Cup 2026 on Monday, with supporters taking to the streets waving Brazilian flags, beating drums and sounding horns after the final whistle.

video:video.video:title: Jordan Chiles on finally meeting Beyoncé
video:video.video:description: Jordan Chiles finally got the moment she's been waiting for: meeting Beyoncé.

video:video.video:title: Explosion seriously injures 3 in Monaco as the suspect flees into France
video:video.video:description: A blast from an explosive device has seriously injured three people at a residential building in Monaco, and the attacker fled to France, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Chinese dissident recounts perilous dinghy escape to South Korea and how he got to Canada
video:video.video:description: Dong Guangping had been locked up in China several times, including for his activities commemorating the 1989 crackdown on pro-democracy protesters in Beijing’s Tiananmen Square and past efforts to flee.

video:video.video:title: Teyana Taylor stunned, Lauryn Hill inspires, at BETs
video:video.video:description: Teyana Taylor is left stunned as Janet Jackson presents her with BET's Icon of the Year Award; Lauryn Hill named Living Legend Icon, and D'Angelo's children introduce a musical tribute to the late star.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians turn to charity kitchens for food as shortages continue to grip the enclave
video:video.video:description: Palestinians crowded around a charity kitchen in northern Gaza on Monday, seeking food as shortages continued to grip the enclave.

video:video.video:title: Heat patrols in Nashville fan out across city to help people dealing with extreme high temps
video:video.video:description: The city of Nashville sent out emergency personnel to hand out water and check on people during one of several days of brutally hot and humid weather forecasted for Tennessee.

video:video.video:title: Beni celebrates first documented recovery from Ebola as new treatment centre is set to open
video:video.video:description: The city of Beni celebrated its first documented Ebola survivor Monday as a new treatment center is set to open in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

video:video.video:title: Global scammers are using US tech to fleece people out of life savings
video:video.video:description: Technology from American companies is being used to power a revolution in the scam industry, playing a key role in the industrialization and globalization of fraud.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians find connection, identity and resilience in traditional embroidery
video:video.video:description: Many in the Palestinian diaspora around the world are engaging with a traditional form of Palestinian embroidery known as “tatreez.”

video:video.video:title: Ari Lennox pays tribute to D'Angelo at BET Awards
video:video.video:description: Ari Lennox says D'Angelo is "the soundtrack to my life." She joined in a tribute performance at the BET Awards.

video:video.video:title: Yodelling festival in full voice despite record temperatures
video:video.video:description: Switzerland's national yodelling festival does not stop for a heatwave. It just moves closer to the fountains.

video:video.video:title: Fire at petrochemical plant in eastern India injures at least 20 people
video:video.video:description: Firefighters used 12 fire engines to bring the blaze under control, while the injured were rescued and taken to nearby hospitals, PTI reported.

video:video.video:title: What a journey - meet Feliks the eagle, back in Serbia after Middle East ordeal
video:video.video:description: He's safe, nestled in the branches now. But Feliks has been on an adventure. The young eastern imperial eagle set off on his first migratory flight last year, only to be caught by poachers in the Middle East and illegally sold.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 29
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, June 29th: Secretary of State Rubio meets with German counterpart in Washington; Flooding makes some roads in Accra impassable; Palestinian officials visit archeological site they say Israel is trying to wrest control of; Music stars among the mourners at the funeral of

video:video.video:title: Date approaches in Iran for massive weeklong funeral of slain supreme leader
video:video.video:description: The date is approaching in Iran for a massive weeklong funeral of its slain supreme leader Ayatollah Khamenei, some four months after he was killed in a war started by the United States and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Shift observed on Trinidad’s shoreline after Venezuela earthquakes
video:video.video:description: Large cracks were seen along the shore on Sunday as residents said that parts of the shoreline, which were previously flat, have since been elevated as high as nearly 20 feet above water.

video:video.video:title: Grief and optimism in scramble to find survivors of deadly quakes in Venezuela
video:video.video:description: A strong aftershock jolted Venezuela early Monday following last week’s devastating back-to-back earthquakes, as civilians and emergency responders kept combing through the ruins of fallen buildings for survivors.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court OKs state laws allowing late-arriving mailed ballots to be counted
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court has ruled that states can count mailed ballots that arrive after Election Day, preserving rules in nearly 30 states, and handing President Donald Trump a significant defeat.

video:video.video:title: Police say 5 people have died in a shooting in Stade in northern Germany
video:video.video:description: Police said the shooting took place in the facility on Dankersstrasse, a street south of the town center. They said that there was no danger to the public.

video:video.video:title: Afghanistan villagers dig through rubble after airstrikes by Pakistan kill 36
video:video.video:description: Overnight ground operations and strikes by Pakistani forces have killed at least 36 civilians and injured more than 160 others, Afghan officials said Monday, as tensions between the neighbors further escalated.

video:video.video:title: Stars arrive for Clive Davis' funeral
video:video.video:description: Barry Manilow, Dionne Warwick, Stevie Wonder, Diane Warren and Adrien Brody were among the mourners arriving for the funeral of Clive Davis, in New York, Monday.

video:video.video:title: Stars arrive for Clive Davis' funeral
video:video.video:description: Barry Manilow, Dionne Warwick, Stevie Wonder, among mourners arriving for the funeral of music mogul Clive Davis, in New York.

video:video.video:title: Europe's heatwave continues, across Romania, Serbia and Croatia
video:video.video:description: mperatures have soared across Europe over the weekend. Almost all of Romania was under a Red Warning for heat, the Romanian meteorology authority said on Monday with temperatures surpassing 40 degrees Celsius (104 Fahrenheit).

video:video.video:title: Heavy rains flood Accra, prompting rescues and a travel warning in Ghana
video:video.video:description: Ghana’s National Disaster Management Organisation (NADMO) carried out operations in Tesano, on the outskirts of the capital Accra, to save civilians from floodwaters Monday morning after heavy rains left much of the city inundated.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s push to toss $5 million verdict in E. Jean Carroll sexual abuse case
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court on Monday rejected a push by President Donald Trump to throw out a jury’s finding that he sexually abused writer E.

video:video.video:title: Jackson, Taylor among stars at BET Awards
video:video.video:description: A-listers including Janet Jackson, Teyana Taylor, and Jamie Foxx stun at the BET Awards in Los Angeles.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 29
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, June 29th: Crews in Venezuela search for earthquake survivors; Iran threatens to break off talks with U.S.; Deadly heatwave in Europe; Wildfire near Colorado-Utah border kills three firefighters.

video:video.video:title: New York City parade helps wrap up LGBTQ+ Pride Month
video:video.video:description: Pride Month celebrations peaked with big parades in New York, San Francisco and some other cities on Sunday. (AP Video by Aron Ranen and production by Vanessa A.

video:video.video:title: Stevie Wonder, Bruce Springsteen, Usher, Alicia Keys leave Clive Davis's funeral
video:video.video:description: The legendary record executive was remembered Monday at the Central Synagogue in New York. Davis died June 22 in his Manhattan apartment at the age of 94. (June 29)

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court rules that Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook can remain in her job
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court has ruled that Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook can remain in her job for now, hobbling President Donald Trump's bid to assert control over the nation's central bank.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: A heat dome will engulf World Cup games in the eastern US this week. Here's what to expect
video:video.video:description: The World Cup games are heating up this week with knockout rounds, and a dangerous heat dome that will engulf the central and eastern U.S.

video:video.video:title: Dogs splash through pool as Mississippi animal shelter weathers extreme heat
video:video.video:description: Amid soaring temperatures, dogs at a Jackson, Mississippi animal shelter with no air conditioning cooled off in a splash pool Tuesday afternoon.

video:video.video:title: UN's World Food Programme distributes food to quake victims in Venezuela
video:video.video:description: The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) increased its operations in Venezuela, distributing food assistance and establishing temporary feeding centres to support affected communities.

video:video.video:title: AI has a huge environmental footprint
video:video.video:description: As the world tries to curb human-caused climate change and not run dry of water, every online query is increasing our environmental footprint and exacerbating the problem.

video:video.video:title: Vic Mensa on his short film 'HALFRICAN'
video:video.video:description: Vic Mensa says his short film "HALFRICAN" was "a labor of community."

video:video.video:title: South Korea coach and team booed by angry fans at airport after World Cup exit
video:video.video:description: Former South Korea head coach Hong Myung-bo and nine of the 26 players in the South Korea squad landed at Incheon International Airport near Seoul under heavy security.

video:video.video:title: Singing and selfies to launch new 'Moana'
video:video.video:description: Dwayne Johnson performs "You're Welcome," as he joins "Moana" stars old and new, surprising fans at a London event celebrating the upcoming live action remake.

video:video.video:title: Leon Thomas: 'R&B is home for me'
video:video.video:description: Leon Thomas explains the impact R&B music has had on his life and artistry. He was honored with the ASCAP Vanguard Award.

video:video.video:title: Inde Navarette says working on 'Obsession' unlocked a newfound confidence within herself
video:video.video:description: Inde Navarrette says working on "Obsession" gave her a newfound confidence in herself. Navarrette's career has skyrocketed her to global fame and established her as a fan-favorite scream queen.

video:video.video:title: Jordan Chiles: 'Women can do anything'
video:video.video:description: Jordan Chiles talks about the importance of support for women's sports. She was nominated for sportswoman of the year at the BET Awards.

video:video.video:title: People experiencing homelessness seek respite from the heat at a cooling center in Georgia
video:video.video:description: People without access to housing are getting a break from the heat at a homeless shelter in Georgia.

video:video.video:title: Bone found in a drawer turns out to be a dinosaur from Antarctica
video:video.video:description: A bone found in a drawer has turned out to be a dinosaur from Antarctica. Researchers compared the fossil to other more complete dinosaur remains and have described it as belonging to the titanosaur group.
video:video.video:tag: Box Butte County Natural History Museum

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 30
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 30: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship; Aid groups warn Venezuela’s healthcare system is near its limit; U.S. envoys arrive in Qatar for Iran war mediation; Sweden and France face-off at World Cup win-or-go-home match.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Morocco fans gather in Monterrey before knockout clash with the Netherlands
video:video.video:description: Morocco supporters gathered at Monterrey's Macroplaza on Sunday, creating a festive atmosphere ahead of their team's Round of 32 World Cup match against the Netherlands.

video:video.video:title: Fans celebrate after Paraguay stuns Germany for the biggest upset of the 2026 World Cup
video:video.video:description: Paraguay fans celebrated in Foxborough, Massachusetts on Monday, singing, dancing and marching with drums, after Paraguay stunned Germany in a penalty shootout for the biggest upset of the 2026 World Cup.

video:video.video:title: How to normalize money conversations with your kids
video:video.video:description: Money talk isn't just for adults anymore. In a world of hidden subscription fees and rising student loan debt, parents are shifting from shielding kids from money to teaching them how to master it.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 30
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 30th: U.S. and Iran appear to have paused strikes; Concern grows for people left homeless by Venezuela earthquakes; Supreme Court rules mailed ballots that arrived late can be counted, with proper postmark; Paraguay beats Germany in World Cup upset.

video:video.video:title: AP/NORC poll: Who flies the US flag, who won’t and what it signals about America
video:video.video:description: New AP/NORC poll indicates Americans’ views of the flag are divided by politics, age and race (AP video: Mike Householder

video:video.video:title: June Diane Raphael talks about working with James Van Der Beek in 'Elle'
video:video.video:description: James Van Der Beek, who died in February after a bowel cancer diagnosis, had a recurring role in Season 1 of the Prime Video series. (June 29)

video:video.video:title: How does the UK get a new Prime Minister without a general election?
video:video.video:description: Recently elected UK lawmaker Andy Burnham has been referred to as "likely Britain's next Prime Minister", following the resignation of current Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court strikes down limits on political party spending, backing Republican appeal
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court has erased limits on how much political parties can spend in coordination with candidates for Congress and president, striking down a federal election law that's more than 50 years old.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court upholds laws barring transgender girls and women from competing on school sports teams
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court has upheld state laws barring transgender girls and women from competing on high school and college sports teams in another setback for transgender rights.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: World Cup buzz has the potential to mint more soccer fans among kids — some just toddlers now
video:video.video:description: Across the country, experts are watching to see whether the World Cup will give a boost to youth soccer and mint fans for decades to come.

video:video.video:title: Roof collapse at tutoring centre under construction in Lahore kills at least 14 children
video:video.video:description: A roof collapse at a tutoring center in Pakistan’s Kahna Town of Lahore District killed at least 14 schoolchildren on Tuesday, according to the police and rescue officials.

video:video.video:title: US Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, rejecting Trump’s proposed limits
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court has rejected President Donald Trump's executive order on birthright citizenship that would have denied citizenship to children born to mothers who are in the country illegally or temporarily.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Trump administration silent on Iranian school strike four months later
video:video.video:description: It was the deadliest reported strike in the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran, and most of the victims were children.

video:video.video:title: Anti-immigration protesters march in South Africa
video:video.video:description: Thousands of demonstrators gathered in parts of South Africa to rally against illegal immigration in the biggest migration-related protests since the wave of anti-migrant violence in 2008.

video:video.video:title: Celebrations erupt at San Diego watch party as Mexico tops Ecuador, 2-0
video:video.video:description: The crowd at a World Cup watch party in San Diego, California erupted in raucous celebrations on Tuesday night as Mexico topped Ecuador 2-0 in a World Cup round of 32 soccer match.

video:video.video:title: Hospital in France gears up for the next heat wave armed with lessons learnt from previous ones
video:video.video:description: At a Paris-region hospital, emergency medics needed it to plunge patients into cold-water baths to speedily bring down their temperatures so they wouldn't join the growing tally of dead from a record-smashing heat wave.

video:video.video:title: Italy displays paintings from an ancient Etruscan tomb, its latest cultural acquisition
video:video.video:description: Italy on Tuesday put on display one of the best known examples of Etruscan painting, from a tomb that has become the latest acquisition in the Culture Ministry’s buying spree of big-ticket pieces of the country’s cultural heritage.

video:video.video:title: 'Kiki's Delivery Service' author says book was inspired by daughter's witch drawing
video:video.video:description: Eiko Kadono, the author of the novel behind Hayao Miyazaki's film "Kiki's Delivery Service," discusses her love of writing and how the book came to be. (July 1)
